page: Amazon Web Services r7i.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 vCPUs mapped to 96 physical cores of a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It has no local storage and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10%. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth for large blocks, although Passmark memory scores sit in the average tier. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contribute to top-tier results in ray tracing, image processing, and cryptographic operations. Single-core performance and LLM inference speeds for text generation generally rank in the strong to average tiers.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7i.48xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, leveraging DDR5 speeds to maximize throughput per allocated resource. However, the absence of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, which may introduce latency compared to instances with local NVMe drives. The instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, heavy multi-threaded compute tasks, and medium-to-large LLM inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores of a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong cryptographic capabilities, aided by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. While single-core speeds and memory latency benchmarks fall into average to strong tiers, its massive memory footprint and multi-threaded capacity make it highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. The r7i.48xlarge is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, enterprise applications, and LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise databases and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors
1536.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Best suited for large in-memory databases, Redis, and LLM inference
